versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: And I asked, “What should I cry out?”
and all its glory like the flowers of the field.
7 The grass withers and the flowers fall
when the breath of the LORD blows on them;
8 The grass withers and the flowers fall,
but the word of our God stands forever.”

context_lines: In **Isaiah 40**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Isaiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Enduring Word** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Isaiah 40** remains the first control on meaning.
